1035.showDesc: The Womack Sisters, BG, Zeimani and Kucha Womack, are a new generation of soul royalty, renowned for their blend of R&B, soul, and gospel influences drawn from their rich family legacy. As granddaughters of the legendary Sam Cooke, daughters of renowned soul and R&B artists Womack & Womack, and nieces of Bobby Womack, they carry on the Womack family’s musical traditions, infusing their work with the soul and passion that has defined the family’s impact on music for generations.

1036.showDesc: Corb Lund has received multiple CCMA, JUNO, and international award nominations and wins. A rural Albertan hailing from the foothills of the Rocky Mountains with a long family lineage of ranchers and rodeo people, Lund is about as authentic as they come. Embracing both his Western heritage and his indie rock past through his music, he has been known to filter a range of cowboy themes past and present through his unique lens.

1037.showDesc: The highly anticipated follow-up to their breakout 2022 self-titled debut, Thee Sacred Soul’s Got A Story To Tell, features 12 original songs, a soaring statement of exquisite craftsmanship from this young band from San Diego, whose own story grows bigger by the day. The group boasts millions of monthly listeners on Spotify, celebrity fans like SZA, Alicia Keys, and Kylie Jenner, an NPR Tiny Desk performance that burned the house down – and all in the first two years of the band’s existence.

1051.showDesc: Please join us on Thursday evening as we gather to open the festival in a good way. We are grateful to the traditional Knowledge Keepers Lyndon Aginas and Dwight Paul, for sharing their teachings, presence, and blessing with us.

1038.showDesc: Irish trio Amble harness a rare magic threaded into the fabric of their novelistic folk. The group of Robbie Cunningham, Ross McNerney, and Oisin McCaffrey have unassumingly gone from quitting their day jobs in rural Ireland to the doorstep of an international breakthrough buoyed by over 100 million streams and sold-out shows around the world. Their relatability, intimate storytelling, and authenticity marry seamlessly with no-frills acoustic instrumentation, calm-inducing soundscapes, and heavenly natural harmonies.

1039.showDesc: Buffalo Traffic Jam is a band born in the heart of Bozeman where its members first crossed paths as students at Montana State University. United by a love for the raw beauty of acoustic sound, they began crafting music that evokes wide-open landscapes and the emotions that live quietly beneath the surface of everyday life. The result was a self-titled debut EP followed by singles, “Rescue Me” and “Forgot Your Roots,” the latter of which saw the band gain their first bit of online momentum, leading to explosive growth in the past year.

1040.showDesc: Of Monsters and Men went from the talk of Iceland to the top of the global conversation on their 2011 debut, My Head Is An Animal, earning them performances at festivals such as Coachella, Lollapalooza, Glastonbury, and beyond. Their 2025 album, All is Love and Pain in the Mouse Parade, “shows off an emotional depth and darkness not necessarily associated with the ‘Little Talks’ party starters. It’s more introspective, the band say, and the vocals of Nanna Hilmarsdóttir allow their message to hit with a proper punch.” (Rolling Stone)

1050.showDesc:
Enjoy more than 2 hours of sessions and concerts across 6 side stages. Take in concerts with Dove Ellis, Mariel Buckley, SYML, The Mbira Renaissance Band, The Milk Carton Kids, and The Pairs, as well as sessions featuring Brianna Lizotte, Fantastic Cat, School of Song (Caylie G, Mitch Gorman, Paul Cournoyer, The Western Thistles), Hurray for the Riff Raff, Joe Nolan, Madalitso Band, Mia Kelly, Rockin' Dopsie Jr. & The Zydeco Twisters, Sister Ray, and SupaLung.

Full Session Stage schedule will be available in the coming weeks.
Lineup subject to change.

1332.showDesc: Unapologetically genuine, wry and soft-hearted, Mariel Buckley has planted her flag firmly as a songwriter who’s in it for the long haul. Raised in the heart of Canada’s rodeo and country music scenes, Buckley was destined to challenge the boundaries around her, both in life and in song. Written largely during a reflective period in Edmonton and finished in Nashville, her latest album, Strange Trip Ahead, is a meditation on the push and pull of creative life — the exhaustion and exhilaration of pursuing meaning through art.

1338.showDesc:
School of Song (Canada/AB)

Caylie G (they/she) is a queer artist based in Edmonton, Alberta, on Treaty Six Territory. With roots firmly planted in their prairie upbringings and a soft spot for glittering theatrics, Caylie has cultivated a sound best described as sparkly cowboy-pop. Their music explores the valleys and swells of love, heartbreak, and the complexities of humanness and queerness with a tender balance of vulnerability and strength. Caylie’s lyrics resonate with folks from all walks of life, capturing the essence of the human experience with softness and candour.

Mitch Gorman is an emerging indie-folk singer-songwriter from Grand Prairie, Alberta. Known for his heartfelt lyrics and captivating melodies, Mitch draws inspiration from a blend of folk and alternative influences, channeling the essence of artists like Noah Kahan, Bon Iver, and Gregory Alan Isakov.

With 27k followers on TikTok, Mitch has cultivated a dedicated fan base through his engaging content, which features both original songs and creative covers. His cover of Tenerife Sea by Ed Sheeran has garnered over 1M views, earning recognition from Sheeran himself, who commented, “Nailed it.” In addition, several of his other TikTok videos have reached over 500K+ views, demonstrating his growing popularity and ability to resonate with listeners.

Paul Cournoyer has built a body of work around the tension between sound and silence, presence and restraint. Marrying acoustic and electronic, weaving words, languages, and rhythms, his music moves fluidly between folk, rock, and pop without ever feeling rushed. An Edmonton native and member of bilingual folk duo Post Script, Cournoyer delivers his solo work in French as an ode to his Franco-Albertan roots and community.

His most recent album, Victoires et Défaites, is the clearest expression of that instinct yet: a rich, nuanced record rooted in the folk tradition but never confined by it, connecting heart and mind one song at a time.

Blending old-time roots with modern-day storytelling, The Western Thistles are an Edmonton folk duo known for their clawhammer banjo, rhythm guitar, and rich vocal harmonies. The group was born from the musical connection between Mark Boer and Sabrina (Bri) Huot-Boer, who came together to weld their talents for the sake of the song. Their sound draws from folk, roots, and old-time traditions while carving out a space distinctly their own. 

In 2025, The Western Thistles released their self-titled debut album, recorded live off the floor and co-produced by Miles Wilkinson (Guy Clark, Emmylou Harris, Darrell Scott). The album reached #26 on CKUA’s Top 100 most-played albums of the year, standing as a testament to their commitment to crafting songs with heart, grit, and intention.

As songwriters and performers, they channel lived experience into work that reflects both personal narrative and musical lineage. Rooted in a dedication to craft, they shape songs that foster connection, creating space for listeners to hear their own stories reflected in the music.
tags: School of Song (Caylie G, Mitch Gorman, Paul Cournoyer, The Western Thistles)

school-of-song-caylie-g-mitch-gorman-pau.name: School of Song - Caylie G, Mitch Gorman, Paul Cournoyer & The Western Thistles

1346.showName: Rockin' Dopsie Jr & The Zydeco Twisters Concert
1346.showDesc: Rockin' Dopsie, Jr. & The Zydeco Twisters, are referred to as "Zydeco's Royal Family". For seven decades the high-energy stage name Rockin’ Dopsie (pronounced Doopsie) has stood tall in the deep-groove world of zydeco. In addition to his famous footwork, Dopsie’s performance presence is further fueled by his fleet-fingered playing of the rub-board. Dopsie’s percussive prowess both pushes and is propelled by the ferocious grooves fired up by the Zydeco Twisters.
tags: Rockin' Dopsie Jr & The Zydeco Twisters

rockin-dopsie-jr-the-zydeco-twisters.name: Rockin' Dopsie Jr & The Zydeco Twisters

1353.showDesc: SYML is the solo venture of artist Brian Fennell. Welsh for “simple”, SYML makes music that taps into the instincts that drive us to places of sanctuary. His self-titled first release, included the multiplatinum song, “Where’s My Love” and the Gold Single fan favourite, “Girl." His sophomore album, The Day My Father Died, was described by NPR as “euphoric,” and featured collaborations with Lucius, Sara Watkins, and Charlotte Lawrence. SYML is also known for his presence across genres, including a feature on Lana Del Rey’s song “Paris, Texas,”, and contributions to songs by the UK-electronic artist George Fitzgerald and Colombian artist Elsa Y Elmar.

1358.showDesc: Their sibling harmonies and familial banter quickly connect them to their audience. Whether The Pairs are playing an intimate house concert or a sold-out show, these classically trained vocalists make it their mission to use three-part harmony and honest storytelling as powerful tools that soften and connect us. “I first saw The Pairs at a folk festival do a rare thing - make a noisy, wandering crowd suddenly go quiet. You could tell right then they are something rare and one of the brightest new voices in Canadian folk.” (Tom Power, CBC)

1367.showDesc: Dove Ellis is a songwriter and performer from the west of Ireland. In the UK and Ireland's ever-accelerating post-Covid music scene, genres are dissolving faster than ever before, and Dove is a prime example of this shapeshifting next-Gen. His writing covers a range of styles — from soft acoustic ballads to hammering bass lines to visceral, ambient soundscapes — all kept tight and cohesive behind his high tenor croon. Cutting his teeth in Manchester's flourishing live scene, Dove has been spreading word-of-mouth after notable performances at End of the Road, support slots for Fabiana Palladino and dimly-lit headline shows in crumbling mansions. Dove has recently released his hotly anticipated debut single, ‘To The Sandals’, getting international plaudits from Stereogum, The Fader, So Young & will be supporting Geese on their North American tour this fall.

1041.showDesc: Grand Ole Opry members and Grammy nominated, Dailey & Vincent, are among the most respected and celebrated duos in American roots music. With unmatched harmonies, masterful musicianship, and a signature blend of bluegrass, country, and gospel, Jamie Dailey and Darrin Vincent have built a career defined by excellence, authenticity, and heart. In 2026, they reached a major milestone with “Ruby,” a multi-week No. 1 song on the Bluegrass Today chart.

1042.showDesc: Leif Vollebekk is an acclaimed singer-songwriter originally from Ottawa who now resides in Montreal. Vollebekk has sold-out headlining tours across North America, Europe and Australia. His 2017 release, Twin Solitude, was a breakthrough album, landing a spot on the Polaris Music Prize shortlist, a JUNO nomination and gathering over 60 million streams. “[His] songs […] are pensive and restless, blossoming with stream-of-consciousness verses that bear the marks of long nights spent alone on the road.” (The New York Times)

1043.showDesc: Annie & The Caldwells are a family that plays a powerful disco soul from West Point, Mississippi. Their latest album embraces the idea that “You don’t need to share the Caldwells’ faith to find something powerful and inspiring in [their lyrics], particularly given the current climate, which can easily incline you towards hopelessness; something steeped in tradition seems apropos right now. You should listen to Can’t Lose My (Soul) purely on musical terms. Moreover, it’s an album you might need.” (The Guardian)

1044.showDesc: Emerging as true trailblazers in the hip-hop scene, since 1991 Arrested Development stands as a cultural beacon of consciousness and empowerment worldwide. Their latest project, Adult Contemporary Hip-Hop, showcases their innovative spirit, unmatched lyricism, soulful storytelling, and profound awareness, exemplifying the unique artistry that only Arrested Development can provide.

1045.showDesc: Cat Power’s seventh studio album, The Greatest proved to be an immediate landmark in her already acclaimed career upon its 2006 release. Named by Rolling Stone at #6 on its “Top 50 Albums of 2006” as well #26 on its “100 Best Albums of the 2000s,” the collection was awarded with 2006’s prestigious Shortlist Music Prize, making history as the first album by a female artist to be accorded that honour.  The festival is honoured to celebrate the 20th year of one of Cat Power’s most iconic albums.

1073.showDesc: Dana Sipos coaxes silkworms out of their cocoons and spins them into song. She inhabited the far north before going nomad and her captivatingly nuanced songs are filled with a wild wind and a haunting surrealism. She now resides among the old growth of Vancouver Island. A consummate performer, Sipos has brought her unique brand of cosmic folk to living rooms, concert halls and festival stages across North America, Europe, Australia and New Zealand.

1297.showDesc: Anna Tivel is a songwriter who is forever drawn to the quiet stories of ordinary life. Tivel has been likened to a short story writer and praised by NPR, Rolling Stone, Billboard, and others for her keen observations of the human condition. "Anna Tivel is just one of my absolute favourite living songwriters. Her writing on [Outsiders] is at the same level as Paul Simon when he wrote 'The Boxer' and 'American Tune.’” (Ann Power, NPR)

1299.showDesc: Bella White is a truly one-of-a-kind musical talent: a finespun breed of country-folk both fresh in perspective and wholly steeped in bluegrass tradition. “Her coos and the breaks in her voice swell empathy and commiseration in the audience, and whether you’re a young adult going through the same struggles of finding where you should to be and with whom, or an older person reminiscing on that era in your own life, the music of Bella White finds deep appeal.” (Saving Country Music)

1300.showDesc: Brianna Lizotte is a JUNO nominated fiddle player from Edmonton. She grew up in a household where music was constantly played, whether it was from home videos or kitchen parties. Brianna’s two albums, Scratch ‘Em, and her newest project, Winston & I, have received radio play on CBC Radio, CJSR, CFWE, and appeared on CKUA’s Top 30 chart. She can also be found teaching Metis history through music and dance workshops to students across the province.

1303.showDesc: From her 2016 breakthrough album, Honest Life, to 2020’s Grammy-nominated, Old Flowers, Courtney Marie Andrews has been celebrated as an artist who challenges herself, and who finds new interplays of Folk and Americana. According to Andrews, her latest release, Valentine, is “a record in pursuit of love”. But love, it turns out, “is a lot more than I gave it credit for,” she explains. “It’s built over years, it’s built with trust, with changes, it becomes something new and unrecognizable, the deeper you go.”

1304.showDesc: Guitarist, vocalist and songwriter D.K. Harrell, one of the most formidable young stars of the current blues resurgence, sounds like no one else on the scene today. With his stinging, single-note string bending, deep soul vocals, and attention-grabbing original songs, Harrell is already in a league of his own. “With every exuberant turn of phrase, and each sharp guitar lick, the Louisiana born showman displays a pure joy in performing that’s hard to resist. …Ultimately, Harrell’s greatest talent is just being his own larger-than-life self.” (No Depression)

1305.showDesc: Whether he appears solo or with his full band, Daby Touré never fails to astound audiences with his ethereal voice, masterful guitar work and catchy original songs of “global soul-tinged pop.” (BBC) Most recently, Daby composed and recorded the soundtrack for the 2025 documentary, La Sang et La Boue (Of Blood and Mud), about the child miners in Congo who mine for coltan, a sought after mineral used in our cell phones. The music is as beautiful as is the scenery where the film was shot, accompanying an incredibly hard and important story.

1308.showDesc: With Dankoraba, her 2025 JUNO award winning album, Djely Tapa pursues her artistic vision at the crossroads of Malian and Western cultures, past, present and future. Originally from the Kayes region of western Mali, Tapa shares the wise words and vocal art of the griots, emblematic figures of ancient and contemporary Malian society, whose origins date back to the 13th-century Mandingo Empire. Her repertoire blends Mandingo traditions, desert blues and electronic sounds.

1316.showName: Hurray for the Riff Raff Concert
1316.showDesc: Hurray for the Riff Raff founder, Alynda Segarra, has been something of a modern Huck Finn, an itinerant traveler whose adventures prompt art that reminds us there are always other ways to live. Born in the Bronx and of Puerto Rican heritage, Segarra was raised by a blue-collar aunt and uncle, where they were radicalized before they were a teenager, baptized in the anti-war movement and galvanized in New York’s punk haunts and queer spaces. They steadily rose from house shows to a major label, where Segarra became a pan-everything fixture of the modern folk movement.

1317.showName: If You Could Read My Mind

1322.showDesc: Jeremie Albino’s songs are grounded in the gritty storytelling of classic country music, propelled by the rhythms of old-school R&B, played with the wild abandon of early rock ’n’ roll, and sung with the deep feeling of southern soul. Thanks to his sweaty, livewire concerts, he has been steadily growing his audience from a few passersby to packed houses around Canada and the United States.

1323.showDesc: A virtuoso guitarist, elusive poet, and musical chameleon, Joe Nolan was raised in the rural community of Sturgeon County, and has always lived on the outskirts, being a hard one to pin down. Breaking genres and defying labels, he defines his sound as "...my caravan diaries." Hailed by The Globe and Mail as "an Americana wunderkind to watch out for," Joe Nolan is proving that praise is well-deserved with his newly released album, Luv in the New World, produced by Grammy Award-winner Tyler Chester.

1324.showDesc: For over three decades, Joel Plaskett has proven himself a spinning wheel of reinvention. The Nova Scotia songwriter's eclectic body of work reveals a restless and playful spirit, always transforming and expanding. His revered career, saw the release of, Songs From The Gang – A Celebration of Joel Plaskett – a deluxe covers album featuring the music of Joel Plaskett performed by Arkells, Sloan, Jill Barber, The Sheepdogs, Julian Taylor, Jenn Grant, Bahamas, City and Colour, Rose Cousins, Frank Turner, and many more.

1326.showDesc: Award-winning, Toronto-based singer-songwriter Julian Taylor has been part of the musical fabric and landscape in Canada for two decades. He enjoyed a breakthrough year in 2020, when his second solo acoustic album, The Ridge, earned more than five million plays on Spotify, and praise from press worldwide. In addition to winning “Solo Artist of the Year” at the Canadian Folk Music Awards, The Ridge was also nominated for two JUNO Awards, the Polaris Prize Long List, and a Summer Solstice Indigenous Award.

1327.showDesc: For over three decades, Kate Rusby has stood as one of the most influential and beloved figures in British folk music. Frequently described as “...a superstar of the British acoustic scene” (The Telegraph), and affectionately hailed as “Folk Royalty” (The Daily), she has carved a singular place in the UK’s musical landscape. Among her many accolades, she has also been awarded an impressive three Honorary Doctorates from three separate universities, all for her service to music, the arts and the preservation of folk song.

tags: Djékady feat. Balla Kouyaté & Mike Block

djekady-feat-balla-kouyate-mike-block.name: Djékady feat. Balla Kouyaté & Mike Block

1334.showDesc:
Join us once again as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival celebrates First Nations, Métis, and Inuit artists through the Nikamowin Program and Indigenous-led programming across the festival site.

We acknowledge that the Edmonton Folk Music Festival takes place on Treaty 6 Territory, the traditional lands of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux, and Métis peoples. We honour the enduring relationships Indigenous Peoples have with these lands and their ongoing contributions to our communities.

We invite festival-goers to gather in a spirit of respect, learning, and celebration as we share Indigenous stories, songs, cultures, and traditions through music, art, and community.

1345.showDesc: Take a 15-time IBMA (International Bluegrass Music Association) Dobro Player of the Year and a Tennessee-born guitar prodigy who made his Grand Ole Opry debut at the age of 11, and you have Rob Ickes and Trey Hensley, a powerhouse acoustic duo that has electrified the acoustic music scene. Known for their white-hot picking, stone country vocals with soul and world class live musicianship, Ickes and Hensley meld together blues, bluegrass, country, rock and other string band music of all kinds to form a signature blend of music that defies restrictions of genre.

1356.showDesc: The Mbira Renaissance Band is one of Edmonton's most exciting groups. Their first full-length 2025 album, Roto, reached the top spot on the CKUA radio charts in its first week. Centered around the soulful sounds of the mbira, a traditional instrument from Zimbabwe, their music offers a fresh, energetic and spellbinding experience of cyclical arrangements, polyrhythm, layered vocals and improvisation. Seamlessly moving between re-interpretations of traditional repertoires and original compositions the band sings songs of family, heritage, celebrations and gatherings.

1357.showDesc: The Milk Carton Kids have remained committed to a deceptively simple idea: music can help turn down the volume on a chaotic world and make room for what matters most. The latest release from the Grammy nominated duo, Lost Cause Lover Fool, is the clearest distillation of that idea yet. With roots-leaning arrangements and a deep trust in space, the album expands the duo’s signature minimalist sound while somehow making it even more focused.

1046.showDesc: Grammy-nominated Nigerien Tuareg guitar hero Bombino, hailed by The New York Times as the "Sultan of Shred", continues to redefine desert blues and rock for a global audience. Over the years, Bombino has collaborated with acclaimed producers such as Dan Auerbach (The Black Keys) on Nomad and David Longstreth (Dirty Projectors) on Azel, earning praise from icons including Keith Richards, Stevie Wonder, Robert Plant, and Arcade Fire.

1047.showDesc: St. Paul & The Broken Bones are a powerhouse soul ensemble from Alabama known for their electrifying live shows and impassioned vocals. The group has shared stages with The Rolling Stones and Black Pumas, and has performed at major festivals like Coachella, Glastonbury, Lollapalooza, and Bonnaroo. Their new self-titled sixth album marks a creative renewal, melding the band’s adventurous spirit with a return to soulful, song-focused roots.

1048.showDesc: Ocie Elliott is the JUNO nominated musical duo of Jon Middleton and Sierra Lundy. The two have come a long way in a short time since a chance encounter in a Salt Spring Island café led to a spark, collaboration, and their ultimate partnership in music. The duo pens tunes that feel lived-in. You can hear their memories, moments, and emotions in the dusty acoustic guitars, the sparse production, and the graceful harmonies between Middleton and Lundy. The duo has been aptly described by one reviewer as “...the aural equivalent of indigo painted skies and the first summer stars."

1049.showDesc: Raised in Hermann, Missouri, Nathaniel Rateliff began his music career playing drums in his family’s church band. “Whether he’s grinding out old-school soul with the Night Sweats or flying solo, he’s obsessed with the idea of authenticity: the idea that a song isn’t a song unless it can be convincingly played alone on an acoustic guitar.” (Pitchfork) Showcasing his true craftsmanship as a songwriter first, it is an honour to feature a solo Ratefill – the perfect punctuation to the 2026 festival.

1296.showDesc: With his sophomore full-length album, Concertos & Serenades, Adam Baldwin offers an east coast testimony that challenges the typical tourism marketing gloss. Through eight masterful yarns, the songwriter bears witness and pays tribute to a tradition of desperation, sinners and losers, perpetual failures, and down-and-out phantoms that haunt his home’s coastlines and back roads - without a passing judgement. Some of the tales happened, some didn’t, and most walk a tightrope between truth and fiction.

1298.showDesc: As one of Iceland’s most successful exports, singer-songwriter Ásgeir has always pushed the boundaries of his thoughtful brand of folk-pop. His debut album, Dýrð í dauðaþögn, became the best-selling debut in Icelandic history, leading to the English-language version, ‘In The Silence’ in 2014.  He continued to refine his sound with albums Afterglow, Bury The Moon, and Time On My Hands, earning praise from press and radio worldwide, including The Independent, MOJO, NME, Clash Magazine, BBC 6 Music and Radio X.

1301.showDesc: Buyepongo, a band known for their infectious Buyangu sound, has become a well-known name in the Los Angeles music scene. Their music is a riotous fusion of influences, seamlessly blending cumbia, hip-hop, punk, funk, and jazz into a deliriously infectious tropical mix that spans the rich tapestry of Latin American culture. This dynamic ensemble has shared the stage with musical heavyweights like Miguel, Warren G, Common, Ozomatli, Celso Piña, Sister Nancy, Sharon Jones and the Dap Kings, and Antibalas.

1306.showDesc: What do you get when an American bluegrass banjo and an Irish tenor banjo join forces? In the case of Coleraine’s Damien O’Kane and California’s Ron Block – fireworks. Widely regarded as two of the greatest exponents of the banjo, they have collaborated to create arguably their own genre, Banjophony, something never heard before that is subtle, awe-inspiring, energetic and majestic.

1307.showName: Djékady feat. Balla Kouyaté & Mike Block Concert
1307.showDesc: Balla Kouyaté and Mike Block met through their work with the Silkroad Ensemble, and have found a simpatico relationship with each other. “Balla’s joy and unbridled enthusiasm is infectious,” Block says. “There is so much trust between us,” offers Kouyaté. “We treat each other like brothers.” Djékady is a Mandinka word with the meaning, “coming together is good.” Every song is a trust fall, with each musician leaping higher and higher knowing their bandmates will catch them every time.
tags: Djékady feat. Balla Kouyaté & Mike Block

djekady-feat-balla-kouyate-mike-block.name: Djékady feat. Balla Kouyaté & Mike Block

1309.showDesc: The duo’s music stems from roots in musical traditions spanning both sides of the pond. In one breath, echoing the great American folk troubadours, and in another, comfortably channeling the elder statesmen of Irish folk. This is no accident. DUG have a shared musical heritage, with members having been born in America and Scotland before arriving in Ireland.

1310.showDesc: Individually, each member of Fantastic Cat boasts their own impressive résumé along with a litany of critical acclaim. Collectively, though, the four transcend their respective roots, emerging as a velvet-clad rock and roll cooperative far greater than the sum of its parts. They say some cats are born fantastic; others have fantasy thrust upon them. These guys are somewhere in the middle.

1314.showDesc: Wales born instrumentalist Gwenifer Raymond is a celebrated champion of the finger-style guitar. She has drawn international acclaim for her repurposing of Mississippi blues and John Fahey’s intricate Americana to embody her roots in rural South Wales. The Guardian has described her as a “profound talent” whilst The Observer has praised her “awe-inspiring technique and intense musicality”, and Uncut Magazine has championed her “fast-developing talents as a composer of eerie menace.”

tags: Djékady feat. Balla Kouyaté & Mike Block

djekady-feat-balla-kouyate-mike-block.name: Djékady feat. Balla Kouyaté & Mike Block

1320.showDesc: Reared in Georgia and settled in North Carolina, Jake Xerxes Fussell has established himself as a devoted listener and contemplative interpreter of a vast array of folk songs, lovingly sourced from a personal store of favourites. On his latest album, When I’m Called, his first as a parent, Fussell returns to a well of music that holds lifelong sentimental meaning, loosely contemplating the passage of time and the procession of life’s unexpected offerings.

1321.showDesc: Jeffrey Martin has been reaching for quiet and stories since he was a kid hiding under the covers with a Discman. He wrote his way through a writing degree, a teaching degree, and years of high school English classes filled with unruly brilliance, all while making records and playing shows whenever he could. Eventually, he left teaching to follow the same dream he encouraged his students to chase. After releasing, Thank God We Left the Garden, he recorded, Alive, July 25, 2025, a no-edits live album captured in Portland with Sam Weber.

1325.showDesc: John R. Miller grew up in Hedgesville, a small town in eastern West Virginia where the mountains meet I-81. Crafting his own blend of laid-back country, blues and folk, he has performed music in nearly all 50 states, Canada, Ireland, the UK, Japan, and much of Europe. His songs have been performed and recorded by several notable artists, including Tyler Childers and David F. Bello. John’s latest album, The Trouble You Follow, “...is about taking all the wrong forks in the road and never being dealt a lucky draw, but deciding to be content and live your life anyway, the mess be damned.” (Saving Country Music)

1328.showDesc: La Déferlance offers a refreshing and brightly coloured take on traditional music. Alongside imaginative arrangements of lively tunes and traditional songs, the band’s repertoire features many original compositions. Their energetic and polished performances are a testament to its members' years of varied musical experience, notably in jazz and classical music. Recipient of numerous nominations, the group now travels around the globe to present its new show, Âmes des bois, created from its second album released in 2025.

1330.showDesc: A dynamic musical duo from Lilongwe, Malawi who are known for their infectious energy and distinctive sound that is created using homemade instruments, Madalitso Band’s music blends Malawian traditions with a raw, rhythmic intensity that captivates audiences worldwide. Their name, “Madalitso,” meaning “blessings,” reflects both their uplifting spirit and the remarkable journey that has taken them from the streets of Malawi to global stages. What began as street performances to survive has evolved into an international touring career, for Yobu Maligwa and Yosefe Kalekeni.

1335.showDesc:
Join us once again as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival celebrates First Nations, Métis, and Inuit artists through the Nikamowin Program and Indigenous-led programming across the festival site.

We acknowledge that the Edmonton Folk Music Festival takes place on Treaty 6 Territory, the traditional lands of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux, and Métis peoples. We honour the enduring relationships Indigenous Peoples have with these lands and their ongoing contributions to our communities.

We invite festival-goers to gather in a spirit of respect, learning, and celebration as we share Indigenous stories, songs, cultures, and traditions through music, art, and community.

1336.showDesc:
Stage 7 comes alive on Sunday morning with special performances by Lyndon Aginas and Darla Daniels.

Join us once again as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival celebrates First Nations, Métis, and Inuit artists through the Nikamowin Program and Indigenous-led programming across the festival site.

We acknowledge that the Edmonton Folk Music Festival takes place on Treaty 6 Territory, the traditional lands of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux, and Métis peoples. We honour the enduring relationships Indigenous Peoples have with these lands and their ongoing contributions to our communities.

We invite festival-goers to gather in a spirit of respect, learning, and celebration as we share Indigenous stories, songs, cultures, and traditions through music, art, and community.

1337.showDesc: Mia Kelly is an award-winning singer-songwriter from Gatineau, Quebec, making her mark on the Canadian folk music scene with a distinctive blend of folk, rock, and blues. Since releasing her 2022 debut album, Garden Through The War, she has been recognized as a poetic lyricist with a powerful and versatile voice. Known for her ability to weave the stories of those around her into her music, Kelly is an imaginative storyteller who channels raw, vulnerable emotion through expertly crafted songs.

1341.showDesc: His songs don’t shout for attention, yet they demand it, with the poetic weight of Leonard Cohen, the introspective fragility of Nick Drake, and the hypnotic, polyrhythmic pulse of West African guitar masters like Ali Farka Touré. He studied music and art in Havana, Cuba, and was an integral part of the Mercury Award-nominated Portico Quartet, before launching his solo career. His album, First Mind, was a remarkable debut that left a lasting impression on all who listened.

1350.showDesc: At some point you confront the fact that you’ve evolved into the person you’ll be forever. Believer, the sophomore effort by Sister Ray, is an expansive folk project led by Toronto-based singer-songwriter Ella Coyes. They evaluate what it means to hold your centre while continually reappraising how to make sense of the world. Conjuring the barbed alternative folk and sweeping Americana of Tom Waits, Smog, and Lucinda Williams, the album unfurls and claws towards a greater emotional precision.

1352.showDesc: SupaLung, a name paying homage to late-'60s rock powerhouse Terry Reid, affectionately known as “Superlungs”, is a firecracker four-piece that’s quickly becoming essential listening for fans of live alt-indie rock who celebrate depth, joy and groove. Fronted by the hauntingly earnest vocals and sharp songwriting of Sam Brookes, and co-piloted by multi-instrumentalist and producer Pete Josef, SupaLung is not just a band, it’s a moment, a feeling, a sonic hug with the heart of a drum that kicks.

1355.showDesc: The Brudi Brothers are a dynamic band hailing from Seattle, who have an inimitable musical and performance dynamic replete with wit and humour. Often recognized as the city's favourite buskers and street poets, they are known for their raucous performances and distinctive old-world soul that stands out in the sphere of modern music. Alongside a touring résumé that includes support for Sierra Ferrell, The Head and the Heart, and Charley Crockett, they gained rapid popularity worldwide when a live clip of their 2025 single, "Me More Cowboy Than You," went viral, garnering over 60 million streams.

1360.showDesc: Despite their influences ranging across the world, The Point. are a band that could only have started in Austin. Fusing psychedelia, Saharan guitar music, and cumbia with jam-band sensibilities, the trio creates a laid-back vibe that’s more than the sum of its parts. Their latest, Maldito Animal (Deluxe Edition), finds the band exploring new directions, the aural equivalent of a record store crate-digger showing off their collection, a panoply of taste culled into one source.

1362.showDesc: Her powerful, soulful voice carries the spirit of her peoples’ songs that have echoed from her homelands since time immemorial, and the musical roots of her family tree run deep. Growing up in a home rich with music and culture, she began singing as soon as she could talk, inspired and nurtured by her parents, siblings, and her dual Plains Cree and Coast Salish heritage. Her 2025 release, “Sage My Soul”, “is Tia Wood’s most impressive vocal performance to date, her voice unabashed and soaring.” (Kelsey Adams, CBC)

1364.showDesc: Trousdale’s debut album, Out of My Mind, earned raves from the likes of the Boston Globe, Consequence, and Atwood Magazine for its powerful songwriting, immaculate harmonies, and ability to bridge gaps between country and indie pop. The group further honed those strengths on the road, finding deeper grooves and learning how to meticulously highlight each of their voices. Their 2025 album, Growing Pains, stares down all the weight of its title, a trio of strong, independent women facing the threats of loneliness, broken hearts, loss, and more, and converting it into transformative, uplifting music.
